b' A Chris Wallace Reality Check, The Nuzzi Denouement & Nich After Neri
Greetings from Los Angeles and welcome back to In the Room, my private email on the media business, which is enduring the acute pangs of post-election season. Fox News\xe2\x80\x99s audience has surged since Trump\xe2\x80\x99s victory, while CNN and MSNBC are cratering in the ratings as discouraged viewers stick their heads in the sand. On Monday, Fox News accounted for 75 percent of the cable news primetime audience. On Tuesday night, it drew seven times the audience of CNN. This is a reliably fallow period for the losing side, of course\xe2\x80\xa6 but what happens if those audiences don\xe2\x80\x99t come back? \xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c

Greetings from Los Angeles and welcome back to In the Room, my private email on the media business, which is enduring the acute pangs of post-election season. Fox News\xe2\x80\x99s audience has surged since Trump \xe2\x80\x99s victory, while CNN and MSNBC are cratering in the ratings as discouraged viewers stick their heads in the sand. On Monday, Fox News accounted for 75 percent of the cable news primetime audience. On Tuesday night, it drew seven times the audience of CNN. This is a reliably fallow period for the losing side, of course\xe2\x80\xa6 but what happens if those audiences don\xe2\x80\x99t come back ?

\xf0\x9f\x8e\x99\xef\xb8\x8f\xf0\x9f\x8d\xb8 The Grill Room : On the latest edition of the podcast, Axios chief and co-founder Jim VandeHei stopped by to discuss the most pressing storylines coming out of the election: Elon Musk \xe2\x80\x99s ascension and the power of X; our hyper-fragmented media landscape; the Democrats\xe2\x80\x99 Joe Rogan problem, and the dispiriting dearth of great journalists. He also offers a very candid assessment of his alma mater: \xe2\x80\x9c The Washington Post is a hot mess.\xe2\x80\x9d Listen to the latest episode here , and follow The Grill Room wherever you get your podcasts.

In tonight\xe2\x80\x99s email, the real story behind Chris Wallace \xe2\x80\x99s sudden departure from CNN, and what it portends for the beleaguered network as Mark Thompson tees up a new round of layoffs. Moving forward, the network will need to be more creative, and frugal, in how it utilizes most of its talent.

Also mentioned in this email : John Malone, John Santucci , Olivia Nuzzi , Andrew Wilson , Amy Entelis , Pete Hegseth , Dana Walden , Mathias D\xc3\xb6pfner , Greg Maffei , Bob Iger , Ryan Lizza , Hoda Kotb , Nicholas Carlson , Jake Tapper , R.F.K. Jr. , and many, many more\xe2\x80\xa6

A MESSAGE FROM OUR SPONSOR
Seniors are feeling the true cost of drug price \xe2\x80\x9cnegotiations.\xe2\x80\x9d

Instead of saving money, some Medicare patients will pay more for medicines.

Others may not be able to get their medicines \xe2\x80\x93 89% of insurers and PBMs say they plan to reduce access to medicines in Medicare Part D because of the Inflation Reduction Act.

Higher costs and less access. That's not what seniors were promised.

Learn more.

But first\xe2\x80\xa6

  • The Malone-Maffei split : John Malone is planning to spin off Liberty Media\xe2\x80\x99s live entertainment portfolio, including its stake in Live Nation, into a separate business, while keeping Formula One in the core media portfolio\xe2\x80\x94a characteristically pragmatic decision to simplify his investments as he sets up his estate. Meanwhile, Malone is dispensing with Liberty C.E.O. Greg Maffei and taking the reins himself, a move that was probably long overdue given Maffei\xe2\x80\x99s myriad missteps in recent years, from SiriusXM and Pandora to TripAdvisor. Of course, the folks who really deserve credit for Liberty\xe2\x80\x99s success are Live Nation C.E.O. Michael Rapino and Formula One chief Chase Carey .

  • The Iger succession : The hunt for Bob Iger \xe2\x80\x99s successor has, not surprisingly, extended well beyond Burbank: A new report in the Journal says incoming board chair James Gorman is considering several external candidates, including Electronic Arts C.E.O. Andrew Wilson . As I reported back in May 2022, Iger had once tried to recruit Wilson to run ESPN, post- Skipper and pre- Pitaro , and the two subsequently talked about Disney acquiring EA. (Wilson also engaged Brian Roberts , who considered spinning off NBCUniversal, merging it with E.A., and putting Wilson in charge).

    All that said, Wilson is by no means a frontrunner. In fact, several well-placed sources still put their money on Disney entertainment co-chair Dana Walden , despite her lack of experience beyond the company\xe2\x80\x99s core content business, and, yes , despite her donations to Kamala . One plausible scenario I\xe2\x80\x99ve heard: Dana gets appointed C.E.O.; current C.F.O. Hugh Johnston serves alongside her as president and C.O.O.; Iger stays on as chairman in perpetuity (go figure); and Gorman becomes lead independent director. Whatever happens, we\xe2\x80\x99re still a long way out from the transition.

  • The Santucci trials : Much further down the Disney org chart, several ABC News insiders remain concerned about executive editorial producer John Santucci \xe2\x80\x99s likely ascension as the network\xe2\x80\x99s primary conduit to the Trump administration. Santucci, you\xe2\x80\x99ll recall, was the one who quietly gave false assurances to Trump campaign officials that his network\xe2\x80\x99s moderators would not fact-check the candidate during the ABC presidential debate in September. I\xe2\x80\x99m told there have been other occasions in which Santucci brokered concessions with the Trump camp that rubbed his colleagues the wrong way.

    Santucci\xe2\x80\x99s defenders say he is simply an aggressive journalist who is being unfairly maligned by critics envious of his success, though even they acknowledge that he inspires what one source described as an \xe2\x80\x9callergic reaction\xe2\x80\x9d among his peers. Santucci, I\xe2\x80\x99ve learned, has also been the subject of multiple H.R. complaints in recent years related to what current and former ABC News sources describe as aggressive, bullying behavior\xe2\x80\x94all of which have been addressed by the network, which declined to comment on the matter.

  • Secretary Hegseth : Late Tuesday, Trump nominated Fox News host Pete Hegseth to serve as the secretary of defense, a choice that the Times charitably described as \xe2\x80\x9coutside the norm.\xe2\x80\x9d (Politico has a more colorful account of the defense industry\xe2\x80\x99s reaction). Hegseth is a veteran of the wars in Iraq and Afghanistan and, in addition to being a vocal champion of Trump on Fox, has used the platform to defend service members accused of war crimes. One additional detail I learned today about Hegseth\xe2\x80\x99s tenure at Fox News: Around 2017, he had an extramarital affair with one of his show\xe2\x80\x99s producers, Jennifer Rauchet , that resulted in a pregnancy. Hegseth later divorced his second wife and married Rauchet, now Hegseth.

    In any event, Fox gave Hegseth a glowing sendoff, describing him as \xe2\x80\x9can exceptional host on Fox & Friends and Fox Nation\xe2\x80\x9d whose \xe2\x80\x9cinsights and analysis especially about the military resonated deeply with our viewers and made the program the major success that it is today.\xe2\x80\x9d Indeed, it seems Hegseth would have had a bright future at the network had he elected to stay. How his insights and analysis inform his leadership of the Pentagon remains to be seen, but a good field guide comes from his book, The War on Warriors , in which he condemns the \xe2\x80\x9ccultural chaos and weakness\xe2\x80\x9d of the military.

  • The Nuzzi-Lizza denouement : Olivia Nuzzi , the former star New York political journalist and R.F.K. Jr. paramour, has withdrawn her request for a protective order against ex-fianc\xc3\xa9 Ryan Lizza after failing to win a motion to seal the court proceedings. In response, Lizza issued a fiery statement accusing Nuzzi of \xe2\x80\x9cshamelessly\xe2\x80\x9d using \xe2\x80\x9cfalse and defamatory allegations as a public relations strategy,\xe2\x80\x9d only to withdraw her \xe2\x80\x9cfabricated claims\xe2\x80\x9d upon learning that \xe2\x80\x9cher lies would be exposed.\xe2\x80\x9d Anyway, this whole sordid saga already feels like a relic of the 2024 campaign, now in the rearview. Only two questions remain: Will Lizza, who remains on leave from Politico amid an internal investigation, return to that job? And what is Nuzzi\xe2\x80\x99s next act?

  • Insider, outsider : Speaking of second acts, Nicholas Carlson , the former Business Insider editor who left after all the sturm und drang over the site\xe2\x80\x99s plagiarism allegations against Neri Oxman , has reemerged. Yes, Nich left of his own volition, but Axel Springer chief Mathias D\xc3\xb6pfner also wanted to fire him, as both Semafor and the Times have reported. In any event, he\xe2\x80\x99s back now as the founder of Dynamo, a new digital video company that will produce business news videos for social media platforms.

    If you\xe2\x80\x99re dubious about the viability of a business that contends that evergreen premium video explainers can compete with solo content creators on platforms it doesn\xe2\x80\x99t control, well, so was I. But Carlson assures me there\xe2\x80\x99s room for everyone: Dynamo, he says, is \xe2\x80\x9cfor aspirational business enthusiasts who, like us, believe business explains the world. We\xe2\x80\x99ll start with one channel on the major platforms and one show. Each episode will be a feature journalism story. Our goal is to tell great stories that are very popular.\xe2\x80\x9d Good luck!

  • And finally\xe2\x80\xa6 Don Lemon has left X.

And now, on to CNN \xe2\x80\xa6

How Chris Wallace Cut the Cord
The backstory of Wallace\xe2\x80\x99s self-defenestration at CNN\xe2\x80\x94the latest shoe to drop in an era that won\xe2\x80\x99t stop ending.
DYLAN BYERS
On Monday evening, Chris Wallace , the veteran newsman, CNN anchor, and ultimate Washington establishmentarian, called the network\xe2\x80\x99s talent chief Amy Entelis to inform her that he intended to leave at the end of his contract. Minutes later, to the consternation of his bosses and presumably their P.R. department, he gave a reporter at The Daily Beast the green light to publish a precooked exclusive about his departure. The piece asserted that the 77-year-old broadcaster was walking away from a seven-figure contract to find a new home in \xe2\x80\x9cstreaming or podcasting,\xe2\x80\x9d which, as Wallace told the reporter, is \xe2\x80\x9cwhere the action seems to be.\xe2\x80\x9d

The story, with its Onion -worthy headline\xe2\x80\x94 Chris Wallace Quits CNN to Build Future in Streaming \xe2\x80\x94described Wallace\xe2\x80\x99s exit as a \xe2\x80\x9cwatershed moment for cable TV,\xe2\x80\x9d a remarkably rare defection by a lifelong TV veteran who had determined he would rather forgo his lucrative salary and test new arenas than continue to suffer through the cable business\xe2\x80\x99s slow but inexorable decline. Indeed, the piece seemed to portray Wallace as the news industry\xe2\x80\x99s own Adrian Wojnarowski , the star ESPN NBA reporter who recently gave up his own $7 million-a-year gig to run the men\xe2\x80\x99s basketball program at his alma mater in rural northwestern New York. As it happens, Wallace was also making $7 million a year at CNN.
The truth is always more nuanced, of course, though in some cases it\xe2\x80\x99s also more simple. As every veteran of this industry knows, almost no one gives up the anchor chair of their own volition: not Hoda Kotb , who only determined she\xe2\x80\x99d reached \xe2\x80\x9cthe top of the wave\xe2\x80\x9d at NBC\xe2\x80\x99s Today show after learning that she\xe2\x80\x99d be asked to take a pay cut ; not Norah O\xe2\x80\x99Donnell , whose desire to pursue more Pope Francis -style interview specials was spurred on by CBS\xe2\x80\x99s perplexing decision to dispense with a star-led strategy at Evening News ; and certainly not Chris Wallace. Yes, everyone wants to spend more time with their families or write new chapters. But, as a wise media executive once told me, \xe2\x80\x9cthey also don\xe2\x80\x99t want to work for less money even though they have already made a fortune.\xe2\x80\x9d

In Wallace\xe2\x80\x99s case, CNN C.E.O. Mark Thompson was planning to cancel both of his shows: Who\xe2\x80\x99s Talking to Chris Wallace , the weekly interview series that was originally supposed to air on CNN+ and now airs on Max\xe2\x80\x94until Friday, when it will air its final episode\xe2\x80\x94and the Saturday morning panel discussion program, The Chris Wallace Show . Instead, Thompson and Entelis intended to offer Wallace a new role as an analyst, which would have resulted in a significantly diminished compensation package and, presumably, far less air time. The CNN leaders conveyed that news to Wallace\xe2\x80\x99s agents, though Wallace maintains that he never engaged in any discussions about a future role because he\xe2\x80\x99d already set his course. \xe2\x80\x9cIt doesn\xe2\x80\x99t matter what was or wasn\xe2\x80\x99t said in that meeting because I had already decided with my wife six months ago to leave CNN,\xe2\x80\x9d Wallace told me in a text message. \xe2\x80\x9cAny further speculation is irrelevant.\xe2\x80\x9d

Regardless, Thompson\xe2\x80\x99s decision to cancel his shows was obviously the right one. The most recent broadcast of The Chris Wallace Show drew 450,000 viewers and just 85,000 in the advertiser-coveted 25-to-54-year-old demo\xe2\x80\x94an audience size at which it\xe2\x80\x99s increasingly hard to justify most of the talent salaries at CNN. While the network will continue to handsomely compensate its major franchise players\xe2\x80\x94 Jake Tapper isn\xe2\x80\x99t going anywhere, for instance\xe2\x80\x94it will need to be more creative, and more frugal, when it comes to highly compensated commentators like Wallace. Ever the diplomat, Thompson issued a graceful statement on Monday calling the former Fox News Sunday host \xe2\x80\x9cone of the most respected political journalists in the news business.\xe2\x80\x9d

As I reported last week, Thompson is on the verge of implementing a new round of layoffs that will target hundreds of employees, after which he plans to create new positions on the digital side of the business. Inevitably, those cuts will include some high-priced TV talent, as well. Wallace would like the world to believe that he got out before the storm hit. In truth, he was one of its first and most obvious casualties.
FOUR STORIES WE\xe2\x80\x99RE TALKING ABOUT
Warners\xe2\x80\x99 I.P. War
The latest twist in the legal feud over Max\xe2\x80\x99s ER reboot.
ERIQ GARDNER
The Grenell Snub
The blowback as Trump begins stocking his cabinet.
TARA PALMERI
A Cecily Brown Christmas
Shining a klieg light on compelling painter.
MARION MANEKER
Media M&A Murmurs
Assessing the sports media business under Trump.
JOHN OURAND
